category_title: Students with severe hearing impairment and EAL who use physical gesture to demonstrate understanding
student_description: Andrius is a student with severe Hearing Impairment and EAL who struggles with traditional written and verbal tasks, often writing only single sentences and showing high dependence on teacher support. However, he demonstrates profound cognitive understanding when allowed to use physical gestures and drama. He uniquely requires an 'encouraging facilitator' approach that avoids singling him out or providing direct, specific help, allowing him to persevere independently and build confidence.

criteria.criterion_name: Sustained High Expectations via Access Support
criteria.rationale: Viewing Andrius through a lens of 'fixed ability' limits his potential, whereas providing specialized HI/EAL support allows him to tackle the same rigorous work as his peers.
criteria.check: The long-term plan must demonstrate that the student is given the same academic tasks as the rest of the class, supported by specialized access tools (e.g., TA support for HI/EAL) rather than simplified content.

criteria.rationale: Andrius shows startling progress and engagement when traditional academics are shifted toward creative, self-organized drama activities.
criteria.check: The long-term plan must incorporate ongoing creative, drama-focused projects that require peer collaboration and autonomy.

criteria.rationale: Singling Andrius out for differentiated tasks isolates him and reduces his resilience, whereas collective lesson designs foster an inclusive ecology.
criteria.check: The long-term plan must design activities that integrate the student into collective group work, explicitly avoiding separating him or creating individualized, differentiated tracks just for him.
